section_name: Consent of city engineer required before connection may be made.
text: It shall be unlawful for any person to tap or make connection with any storm sewer in the city without having obtained the consent in writing of the city engineer. (Code 1980, § 40-33)
All connections with any storm sewer shall comply with specifications to be furnished by and in accordance with the requirements of the city engineer. (Code 1980, § 40-34)

section_name: Liability for damage resulting from connections.
text: Wheneveraconnectionismadewithanystormsewer,thepersonatwhoseinstanceandfor whosebenefittheconnectionismadeshallbeliableforanydamagesresultingtherefromand shallreplacethestreetandsidewalk,alongandthroughwhichsuchconnectionismade,inas good condition as it was before the work was commenced. (Code 1980, § 40-35)
Itshallbeunlawfulforanypersoninthecitytoconnectanybathtub,sink,pit,drywellor water closet with any storm sewer. (Code 1980, § 40-36)
The owner or occupant of premises with which any storm sewer connection is made shall keep such connection in good order and repair at his own expense. (Code 1980, § 40-37) Chapter 24 SIGNS*

section_name: Signs prohibited on public property except in designated entertainment districts.
text: (a) No signs are permitted at any time on public property such as a city street right-of-way and a utility pole, except for portable signs, specifically permitted, in designated entertainment districts. Portable sign means any on-premise sign which is not affixed to real property in such a manner that its removal would cause serious injury or material damage to the property. (b) Portable signs shall be approved and permitted by the city. A business owner in an entertainment district shall apply for a portable sign permit with the city department of development. A copy of the permit guidelines and restrictions shall be available from the office of the city clerk or on the website for the city at the department of development. (Code 1980, § 16-66; Ord. No. 37-91, art. IV, § 6, 8-6-1991; Ord. No. 44-2012, 6-19-2012) Editor’s note—Ord. No. 44-2012, adopted June 19, 2012, changed the title of

It shall be unlawful for any person to place or have placed around the site of Court Square Fountain, on the fountain, fence or on any part thereof, signs of any sort or description. (Code 1980, § 39-1)

It shall be unlawful for any person to post or paste or otherwise to attach any signboard, bill, advertisement or other paper upon a pole erected by any telegraph, telephone, electric light or power company, or to fire alarm poles or to any tree growing on any street in the city, or to any fixed object on city-owned property. (Code 1980, § 39-2)
It shall be unlawful for any person to attach any bill, advertising matter or other paper to any veranda or the front of any building in the city. (Code 1980, § 39-3)

section_name: Owners and occupants not to permit certain signs on premises.
text: It shall be unlawful for any owner or occupant of premises in the city to permit any fence, house, store, wall or veranda thereon to have pasted, hung or attached thereto any showbill or advertising posters. (Code 1980, § 39-4) Supp. No. 19 CD 24:3

It shall be unlawful for any person to tear down, deface or destroy advertising signs or bulletins in the city without having obtained the consent of the owner of such signs or bulletins, or the owner of the property upon which signs or bulletins are displayed. (Code 1980, § 39-6)

section_name: Injuring or carrying away professional plates and signs.
text: It shall be unlawful for any person to take down, deface or carry away any signboard, plate or card indicating the name, occupation or business of any person in the city. (Code 1980, § 39-7)

(a) Political signs must be at least 30 feet from polling entrances on election day, pursuant to State Code. (b) Political signs may be placed at polling locations no more than 36 hours prior to opening. (c) Political signs may be placed no earlier than eight weeks prior to an election. (d) Political signs must be removed ten days after an election, unless there is a runoff election for those candidates, after which political signs must be removed ten days after runoff election. (e) Political signs are prohibited from being nailed/stapled to trees, fire hydrants, public fences, and utility poles on city right-of-ways, pursuant to City Code. (f) Political signs are prohibited on abandoned properties. (g) Political signs are not permitted in public rights-of-way or on city properties, and noncompliance will result in a $500.00 fine for signs posted illegally. (h) Unlawful removal of political signs is a Class 3 misdemeanor. (Ord. No. 46-2023, 10-17-2023) Supp. No. 19 CD 24:4 Chapter 25 SOLID WASTE* Article I. In General Secs. 25-1—25-30. Reserved. Article II. Collection and Disposal Division 1. Generally
No. 54-72; § 1 of Ord. No. 40-76; and

The following words, terms and phrases, when used in this article, shall have the meanings ascribed to them in this section, except where the context clearly indicates a different meaning: Department means the sanitation department. Garbage means all waste accumulations of animals, fruit or vegetable matter that attend the preparation, use, cooking, dealing in or storage of meat, fowl, fish, fruits, or vegetables; also, tin cans or other containers originally used for foodstuffs. Garbage can means a watertight receptacle or container of substantial metal construction or plastic, having a capacity of not less than ten nor more than 30 gallons, with a tightfitting lid or cover, with not less than one handle on the lid or cover and two handles on the receptacle or container by which same may be conveniently lifted or moved. No cans in excess of 30-gallon capacity will be served. Premises means any dwelling, flat, roominghouse, apartment house, hospital, school, hotel, club, restaurant, boardinghouse, eating place, shop, church, place of business, manufacturing establishment, courthouse, jail, city hall, post office or other public buildings. Residence means single-family dwellings, duplexes, townhouses, apartments and condominiums. Roll-out container means a polyethylene wheeled container equipped with a lid and a handle. Rubbish means all nonputrescible solid wastes, consisting of both combustible and noncombustible wastes such as paper, cardboard, glass, crockery, excelsior, cloth and similar materials. Sanitary landfill means a place for dumping or disposing of garbage and rubbish, operated by the city, or such places designated by the county health officer. Superintendent means the superintendent of the department. (Code 1980, 16-1; Ord. No. 48-84, § 1, 6-19-1984) Cross reference—Definitions generally,
breadcrumb: Art. II. Collection and Disposal > Div. 1. Generally

text: Any person doing any act or thing declared by this article to be unlawful shall be guilty of an offense against the city and, upon conviction, shall be punished by a fine of no less than $1.00 nor more than $200.00, or by imprisonment in jail or by hard labor for no longer than six months, or by both fine and imprisonment. (Code 1980, 16-22; Ord. No. 48-84, § 28, 6-19-1984) State law reference—Limitations on penalties, Code of Ala. 1975, § 11-45-9.
breadcrumb: Art. II. Collection and Disposal > Div. 1. Generally

section_name: Collection and disposal service established; frequency of collections.
text: There is hereby established in the city a garbage and rubbish collection, hauling and disposal service, to be operated by the department. The collection, hauling and disposal of garbage shall be made by the department no less frequently than twice each week in residential areas and as necessary or required, except on Sundays, in all business areas, and the charges hereby imposed are based on such periodic collection, hauling and disposal. Rubbish, leaves, grass, straw, and shrubbery trimmings shall be collected once per week in residential areas. All collection schedules are subject to change due to legal holidays or weather conditions. (Code 1980, 16-2; Ord. No. 48-84, § 2, 6-19-1984)
breadcrumb: Art. II. Collection and Disposal > Div. 1. Generally

section_name: Convenient and safe access to be provided.
text: Where commercial collections are made from private alleys and accessways, such approaches shall be maintained so as not to be a hazard to department personnel or equipment. Failure to comply with such provisions after notification by the city department will cause service to be discontinued until such time as the hazard is corrected. (Code 1980, 16-3; Ord. No. 48-84, § 3, 6-19-1984)
breadcrumb: Art. II. Collection and Disposal > Div. 1. Generally

text: Disposal of garbage by single-family dwellings, duplexes, townhouses and condominiums; property where garbage fee not collected; removal or abatement by city. (a) Roll-out containers for residences. The city will provide each resident with a roll-out container for the purpose of storage of garbage and miscellaneous refuse. This is a requirement for all residents. The resident will be responsible for cleaning of the roll-out container. On scheduled garbage pickup days, the resident will be required to place the roll-out container at the curbside not later than 7:00 a.m. and remove the roll-out container from the curbside not later than 7:00 p.m. of the same day. The city will be responsible for repair or replacement of roll-out containers damaged or stolen through no fault of the resident. Upon proof of disability because of health, age or other reasons, the department will continue to collect garbage from garbage cans placed near the back entrance of such residence unless otherwise designated by agreement with the department. No collection personnel shall enter houses or buildings. Supp. No. 19 CD 25:4 (b) Disposal of garbage in apartment complexes. The city will provide each resident with a roll-out container for the purpose of storage of garbage and miscellaneous refuse. The resident will be responsible for cleaning of the roll-out container. On scheduled garbage pickup days, the resident will be required to place the roll-out container at the curbside not later than 7:00 a.m. and remove the roll-out container from the curbside not later than 7:00 p.m. of the same day. The city will be responsible for repair or replacement of roll-out containers damaged or stolen through no fault of the resident. The department will make every effort to accommodate special circumstances which will prevent each resident from placing the roll-out containers on the curb in apartment complexes; however, if this method is not satisfactory to the owner of the apartment complex, the owner must obtain garbage pickup service from a commercial contractor. (c) Disposal of trash in residential areas generally. All trash, except limbs and hedge cuttings less than four feet in length and less than eight inches in diameter, placed for collection, shall be in 30-gallon containers or bags (tied at the top) and shall be placed behind the curb. Residents will be limited to one cubic yard of trash per scheduled pick up. All collection schedules are subject to change due to legal holidays or weather conditions. Brush, tree trunks and large limbs over four feet in length and eight inches in diameter, and piles exceeding one cubic yard may be picked up on a special schedule as outlined by the superintendent or his agent. Charge for this service will be up to $180.00 per load. Items eligible for this service include but are not limited to stoves, refrigerators, freezers, sofas, water heaters, and other appliances. (d) Disposal of household contents from eviction proceedings. Where owners are required to make household contents available to tenants, all contents must be containerized if placed on the public right-of-way. The owner of the property is responsible for removal of the items. Owners are not authorized to leave furnishings and household items on the curb. They must be removed from the property or placed in a dumpster at the owner's expense. Removal or abatement by the city will be subject to item (e) of this section. (e) Property where garbage fee is not collected. Where a garbage fee is not collected on a property, the owner of property is responsible for the removal of trash and other items. (1) Removal or abatement by city of trash and other items. The owner shall be notified by the sanitation department to remove trash and other items. If the trash and other items are not removed or abated within 72 hours after notice, the trash and other items may be removed or abated by the city at the expense of the owner. (2) Administrative fee. There shall be an administrative fee of $150.00 assessed to the owner, which shall be added to the actual cost of the removal or abatement of trash or other items. (3) Collection of administrative fee and costs. The city may collect the administrative fee and costs of the removal or abatement of trash or other items through court action or any other lawful means. (Code 1980, 16-4; Ord. No. 48-84, § 4, 6-19-1984; Ord. No. 16-2012, 3-20-2012; Ord. No. 56-2014, 9-16-2014; Ord. No. 51-2018, 9-4-2018; Ord. No. 61-2023, 12-19-2023) Supp. No. 19 CD 25:5

text: (a) Small business and commercial units. Small business and commercial units that have garbage requirements not exceeding the capacity of containers issued by the department will be serviced by the department if desired. Time of placement and location of containers by business establishments shall be placed at places to be determined by the department on the days and between the hours designated by the department. Small businesses and com- mercial units have the option of obtaining this service from a commercial contractor. (b) Businesses, apartment complexes, condominiums serviced by contractors. Containers must be placed in the rear of businesses, apartment complexes, and condominiums. The department shall determine the place that container is to be placed. (Code 1980, 16-5; Ord. text: (a) Generally. The city operates a landfill for the purpose of disposing of garbage and trash by the city, commercial contractors, and by persons residing in the county. No toxic materials, hazardous or infectious waste will be accepted or disposed of at any landfill operated by the city. All persons entering city landfill property are required to follow instructions and abide by city landfill rules and regulations. Vehicles entering the landfill willbeweighedbeforeandafterdumpingatwhichtimepaymentwillbemadetothelandfill cashier immediately. (b) Landfill service charges. Effective immediately, vehicles entering the city landfill to dump will be charged at a minimum rate of no more than $10.50 with an additional charge of no more than $2.10 per 100 pounds whenever the waste load exceeds 500 pounds. If the scales are not in operation, the charges will be no more than $14.00 per cubic yard for compacted waste and no more than $11.00 per cubic yard for uncompacted waste loads. Except for commercial waste hauling contractors, the maximum charge for landfill services will be $221.00 per vehicle load. From time to time, at the discretion of the landfill management, earth excavation will be accepted at a minimum charge regardless of the load weight. (c) Special handling charges. Effective immediately, in circumstances where additional labor, equipment and time are required to dispose of the waste or prepare a separate dumping area, a $100.00 charge will be assessed; in addition to such $10.50 minimum and $2.10 per 100 poundweightfeeor$221.00 maximumpervehicleloadwhenapplicable. The feeforshreddedtireswillbe$42.00 perton,andthespecialhandlingfeewillnotbecharged. For a tire to be accepted as shredded, it shall be cut, at a minimum, into four (quartered) pieces. (f) Grease trap waste. Effective October 1, 2008, the fee to dispose grease trap waste will be $1,000.00 per month for a maximum of 425 tons per month with an additional charge of $2.00 per ton for each ton over 425 tons each month. (g) Construction and demolition debris. Effective immediately, disposal fees for inert or constructionanddemolitiondebriswillbenomorethan$11.00 percubicyard. Itemswhich typify construction and demolition debris include: brush and land clearing debris such as logs, stumps, branches, limbs, wood brush as well as tree remains from land clearing Supp. No. 11 CD 25:8 operations and construction or demolition projects. State law references—Solid Wastes Disposal Act, Code of Ala. 1975, § 22-27-1 et seq.; local solid waste management programs, Code of Ala. 1975, § 22-27-47; criminal littering, Code of Ala. 1975, § 13 A-7-29. Private contractors must obtain a solid waste disposal permit to render such services from the superintendent of the department or agent. The superintendent of the department or agent will establish criteria relating to the proper type of equipment to be utilized by private contractors. Private contractors must provide both commercial and residential services and their application for a solid waste disposal permit, and the issuance of the permit, is evidence or intent on the part of holder of a solid waste disposal permit to offer both commercial and residential garbage and rubbish service within the corporate limits of the city and within the police jurisdiction thereof. DIVISION 2. TRUCK TRAFFIC*

The following words, terms and phrases, when used in this article, shall have the meanings ascribed to them in this section, except where the context clearly indicates a different meaning: Bulk container means any dumpster or stationary storage facility placed for the temporarycontainerizationofsolidwasteataplaceofbusiness,multiple-dwellingcomplex, industrial or construction site. Citation means a notice charging the violation of this article which directs anyone in violation to pay the prescribed fine, abatement charges, or to appear in municipal court to answer the charges of violation. Commercial solid waste means manmade solid waste generated by stores, offices, restaurants, cafeterias, shopping centers, fast food establishments, convenience stores, and other nonindustrial sources. Corrective warning notice means a courtesy notice to advise anyone that there may be a violationofthisarticlewhichmayrequirecorrectiveactiononthepartoftherecipientofthe notice. Garbage means putrescible animal or vegetable wastes resulting from the handling, preparation, cooking, and consumption of food. section_name: Sweeping trash, litter, and mud into the street.
text: It shall be unlawful to sweep or push litter, trash, leaves, grass, or mud from sidewalks, onto streets where it will be washed into the city storm drains. Such litter and leaves or grass shall be deposited in a proper receptacle which shall be tightly covered or tied to prevent scattering before pickup. Mud shall, as applicable, be swept or shoveled off the street, curb, and gutters and sidewalk. It shall also be unlawful to wash out concrete mixer truck dump chutes into city storm drains or on public property. (Code 1980, § 16-64; Ord. No. 37-91, art. IV, § 4, 8-6-1991)
breadcrumb: Div. 3. Private Collectors > Subdiv. II. Permit

section_name: Indiscriminate dumping or discarding of litter, trash, junk, solid waste and mud.
text: It shall be unlawful for any person to discard or dump along any street, street median, or road, on or off the right-of-way, any household or commercial solid waste or trash or junk on any private or public property unless disposed of in receptacles provided for public use for the deposit of such material, or in an area designated by the state department of environmental management as a permitted disposal site. Mud at construction activities shall be cleaned without delay from public property. Any person charged with a violation of this section shall be required to appear in municipal court to answer the charge or charges and, upon conviction, shall be fined a minimum of $100.00 and shall be subject to any other penalties provided in

text: All household garbage must be stored in city-furnished roll-out containers so as to prevent scattering of garbage by wind, water, traffic or scavenging animals. (Code 1980, § 16-71; Ord. No. 37-91, art. V, § 1, 8-6-1991)

section_name: Disposal of trash in residential areas.
text: (a) All trash shall be placed for collection at curbside no sooner than 48 hours prior to the designated day for such collection. (b) Loose trash, such as leaves and grass clippings, must be containerized in tightly sealed plastic bags or cardboard boxes, except no bagging will be required from January 1 st to April 15 th each year. Containers shall not exceed 30 gallons. Collections will be made in accordance with current city sanitation department policy. Supp. No. 19 CD 25:15 (c) All trash must be placed at curbside in accordance with current city sanitation department policy. Such policy must be similar to the ordinances pertaining to garbage collection, differing as necessary to accommodate the special requirements of trash collection. (d) Scrap lumber, plaster, roofing, concrete, brick, and sanding dust resulting from the construction, repair, remodeling, removal, or demolition of any building on private property will not be removed by the sanitation department. All such material shall be removed by the owner or the contractor responsible for the accumulation of the material before, during or after construction. (e) It shall be the responsibility of any individual or company doing work on private property to remove from premises all residue and rubbish resulting from such work. (Code 1980, § 16-72; Ord. section_name: Plan for temporary traffic operations during construction in public right-of-way.
text: (a) Purpose of plan. (1) Thepurposeofthisplanistooutlinepropertrafficcontrolmethodsandregulationfor all public and private organizations to follow when temporary disruption of street traffic is required for street repairs or work on utility lines under a street. (2) Whilethemainuseofstreetsistocarryvehiculartraffic,pedestrianscrossthestreets andutilitylinesrununderneaththem.Asthenecessityarisesforworkonutilitylines and street repairs, a conflict occurs between the movement of vehicles and the constructionworkonthestreetinvolved.Toreducethisconflicttoaminimum,certain controls are necessary. (3) It is necessary to control the type of excavation, as to the size of the hole and the location of equipment, and the location of the spoil material obtained through excavation. (4) Streetspaceisataminimumandnomorespaceshouldbeusedforconstructionwork than is absolutely necessary. Through barricading and channelization, the remaining streetspaceisusedtoguidetrafficaroundconstructionworkinthebestwaypossible. (b) Purpose of barricading and channelization. (1) The purpose of barricading and channelization is to: a. Provide for the orderly flow of traffic; b. Reduce hazards for motorists, pedestrians and workers; c. Reduce overall job costs to the public; and d. Improve public relations. (2) By using advanced warning signs and traffic cones in addition to barricades, the best type of orderly flow under existing conditions can be maintained. Orderly traffic flow makes it safer for the workman, the pedestrian and the motorist. (3) Goodbarricadingmayincreasetheconstructioncostslightly,buttheoverallresultwill be less delay and less cost to the motorists, as well as less delay and less cost to the contractor. (4) Adequate signs to explain why work in the street is being performed and good barricading are great assets to the public. If the public understands why it is being delayed, there is likely to be less objection to being delayed. (5) It should be noted that construction or repairs in streets create hazardous conditions which can result in traffic accidents if proper precautions are not taken. Public and privateagenciescanbeliableforsuchaccidentsthroughgrossnegligence.Goodsigns, barricading and channelization around hazardous worksites in the streets are deter- rents to such accidents. (6) Whileitisimportanttoprovideadequatebarricadingonresidentialandminorstreets, the most important location for proper channelization is on arterial streets and particularly at signalized intersections. Arterial streets serve the general public, particularly in the morning and evening during the peak-hour flow of traffic. (7) Thepeak-hourinthecityisfrom 7:00 to 9:00 a.m.and 4:00 to 6:00 p.m.Duringthese times construction work will not be allowed on arterial streets, except during emergency conditions or with the approval of the traffic engineering and street maintenance departments. (c) Procedure for obtaining permission to work in city streets. In order for the contractor to close a street partially or completely for construction work, it is necessary to obtain the approval of the traffic engineering department and the street maintenance department. For largerjobsthisisdonegenerallybyaconferencebetweenthedepartmentsandthecontractor priortotheworkbeingstarted.Itmaybenecessaryduringtheprogressionoftheworktohold severaladditionalconferences.Inadditiontotheseconferences,thecontractormustcallinthe street closure to the traffic engineering department 24 hours in advance for the closing of minor and residential streets, and 48 hours in advance for closure involving signalized intersections and arterial streets. (d) Flagman or police officer. (1) If traffic congestion warrants it or if the inspector on the job determines it to be necessary, a contractor will provide flagmen as required for adequate traffic control. Flagmenaremandatoryatintersectionswhereequipmentisintermittentlyblockinga trafficlaneorwhereonlyonelaneisavailablefortwodirectionsoftraffic.Oneflagman is required for each direction of traffic affected. (2) The availability of free police manpower for assistance in construction traffic work is generally limited to emergencies only, as their services are required elsewhere. Off-duty police officers may be employed directly by the contractor, however, to maintain traffic control. For information, call the police department. (3) The traffic engineering department, the police department and the fire department may determine if an emergency exists which will require on-duty police officers to maintain traffic control at a project site. (e) Complete street closure. (1) Theclosingofanentirestreetobstructstraffictothemaximumand,ofcourse,requires detours. If it is found necessary to close any street, the traffic engineer must be consulted for the layout of detour routes and other important information. It is required that every intersection along the detour be marked until the diverted traffic STREETS, SIDEWALKSAND OTHER PUBLIC PLACES

section_name: Public employees to obey traffic regulations.
text: The provisions of this chapter shall apply to the driver of any vehicle owned by or used in theserviceofthe United Statesgovernment,thisstate,county,orcity,anditshallbeunlawful for any such driver to violate any of the provisions of this chapter, except as otherwise permitted in this chapter or by state statute. (a) The driver of an authorized emergency vehicle, when responding to an emergency call orwheninthepursuitofanactualorsuspectedviolatorofthelaworwhenrespondingto,but notuponreturningfrom,afirealarmmayexercisetheprivilegessetforthinthissection,but subject to the conditions stated in this section. (b) The driver of an authorized emergency vehicle may: (1) Park or stand, irrespective of the provisions of this chapter; (2) Proceed past a red or stop signal or stop sign, but only after slowing down as may be necessary for safe operation; (3) Exceed the maximum speed limits so long as he does not endanger life or property; (4) Disregard regulations governing direction of movement or turning in specified direc- tions. (c) The exemptions granted in this section to an authorized emergency vehicle shall apply only when such vehicle is making use of an audible signal meeting the requirements of Code of Ala. 1975, § 32-5-213 and visual requirements of any laws of this state requiring visual signals on emergency vehicles. (d) The provisions of this section shall not relieve the driver of an authorized emergency vehicle from the duty to drive with due regard for the safety of all persons, nor shall such provisions protect the driver from the consequences of his reckless disregard for the safety of others. (Ord. No. 54-72, § 3-7; Code 1980, § 25-25) State law reference—Similar provisions, Code of Ala. 1975, § 32-5 A-7.

section_name: Permission of police required prior to sending ambulance on emergency call; manner of operation on calls.
text: (a) Itshallbethedutyofanypersonoperatinganambulance,uponreceivinganemergency call, to first report such call to the desk at the police headquarters in the city and obtain the verbalpermissionofsuchdesksergeantbeforesending,permittingordrivinganyambulance toanswersuchemergencycall.Itshallbethedutyofthedesksergeantatpoliceheadquarters, upon receiving a request for permission to send or drive any ambulance to answer an emergency call, to immediately give permission therefor, unless an ambulance has already been permitted to go to the scene of the same accident or emergency, in which case the desk sergeant shall refuse permission for another ambulance to answer such call unless he is convincedthatmorethanoneambulanceisnecessarytoproperlytakecareoftheemergency. (b) It shall be unlawful for any person to send, drive, permit or allow any ambulance to answer an emergency call without first obtaining permission from the desk sergeant as provided in this section. (c) It shall be unlawful for any person driving an ambulance on an emergency call within the city limits to exceed a speed of 40 miles per hour. Such person must, at all street intersections, have such ambulance under absolute control. (d) Anypersonviolatinganyoftheprovisionsofthissectionshall,uponconviction,befined not less than $100.00 nor more than $200.00 or sentenced to hard labor for the city not exceeding six months, one or both at the discretion of the municipal judge trying the case. (a) Thecitypolicedepartmentshallimplementanautomobiletheftpreventionprogramto operate as follows: (1) Motor vehicle owners who desire to enroll their vehicles in the automobile theft prevention program shall execute consent forms which will authorize the owners involvementintheprogram.Aconsentformsignedbyamotorvehicleownerprovides authorization for a law enforcement officer to stop the vehicle when it is being driven between the hours of 1:00 a.m. and 5:00 a.m., provided that a decal is conspicuously affixed to the bottom left corner of the back window of the vehicle to provide notice of its enrollment in the combat automobile theft program. (2) The combat automobile theft program decals shall conform to the specifications establishedbythestatedepartmentoflawenforcement.Thedecalsshallbeyellowin colorandwillbeissuedtoownerswhoenrollintheprogram.Thedecalshallbeplaced on the bottom left corner of the back window of the automobile. (3) An administrative fee of $2.00 shall be established to cover costs of the program. The police department shall collect the fees from all program participants. (b) The vehicle owner shall be responsible for removing the decal when terminating participation in the program, or when selling or otherwise transferring ownership of the vehicle. (c) No civil liability will arise from the actions of a law enforcement officer when stopping a vehicle with a yellow decal, evidencing enrollment in the program, when the driver is not enrolled in the program, provided that the stop is made in accordance with the requirements of the combat automobile theft program. (d) This program shall be implemented upon issuance of written program materials and procedures by the police department. (Ord. No. 47-2000, § 1, 12-19-2000)

section_name: Railroad trains not to block highways or streets.
text: (a) It shall be unlawful for a railroad train to be operated so as to prevent the use for vehicular or pedestrian traffic of any public highway or street within the city for a period of time in excess of five minutes. section_name: Use of hand-held wireless communications devices.
text: (a) Definitions.Forthepurposesofthissection,thefollowingwordsandphrasesshallhave the meaning ascribed thereto: Hand-held wireless communication device means a wireless communication device which requires physical manipulation of the device to send or receive data. A hand-held wireless communications device shall include a hands-free wireless communications device which is manually manipulated by the driver in the same manner as a hand-held device. Hands-freewirelesscommunicationsdevicemeansawirelesscommunicationsdevicewhich isactivatedbytheuserwithoutbeingphysicallymanipulatedbytheuserandwhichdoesnot have to be held to the driver's ear. Wireless communications device means a full-duplex device which uses cellular or satellite technology to transmit and receive electronic data in either digital or analog format which is converted by the device to either voice, text, or graphic communication.Awireless communi- cationsdeviceshallincludeattachments,add-ons,orbuiltinfeatureswhichallowacarstereo receiver, or components thereof, to be used as a two-way communications device. (b) Violation.Itshallbeandisherebydeclaredunlawfulandshallbeaseparateoffensefor any person within the corporate limits or police jurisdiction of the city to use a hand-held wirelesscommunicationdevicewhilecommittinganyviolationincludedinthe Alabama Rules of the Road Act (§ 32-5 A-1 et seq.). Use of a hands-free wireless communications device, as defined herein, shall not be a violation of this section. Any person found to have been in violation of this section shall be punished as follows: (1) Anypersonfoundtohavebeeninviolationofthissectiononanyoneoccasionshallbe punishedbyafineofnotmorethan$50.00,orbyimprisonmentinthecityjailfornot more than ten days; (2) Anypersonfoundtohavebeeninviolationofthissectiononasecondoccasionwithin a 12-month period shall be punished by a fine of not more than $100.00, or by imprisonmentinthecityjailfornotmorethantendays,orbyanycombinationofsaid fine and imprisonment; Supp.No.5 CD 27:13 (3) Any person found to have been in violation of this section on a third or subsequent occasion within a 12-month period shall be punished by a fine of not more than $500.00,orbyimprisonmentinthecityjailfornotmorethanthreemonths,orbyany combination of said fine and imprisonment. (c) Exceptions. A person operating a motor vehicle shall not be found to have been in violation of this section where that person is: (1) Using a hand-held wireless communications device as a global positioning or naviga- tion system where the device is affixed to the motor vehicle at the time of its use; (2) Using hand-held wireless communications device to obtain emergency assistance to report a traffic accident, medical emergency, traffic hazard, or a crime—whether the crime has been committed, is in the process of being committed, or has recently been committed—orwherethepersonoperatinganautomobilehasareasonablebeliefthat a person's life or safety is in immediate danger; (3) An operator of an authorized emergency vehicle and engaged in the line and scope of his or her duties; (4) An employee of a company providing utility services to the public and engaged in emergencyresponseorrepairservicesduringorimmediatelyafterautilityemergency which poses a threat to human life or public safety; or (5) Anoperatorofanamateurradioduringanaturalorman-madedisasteroremergency whereotherwiredandwirelesscommunicationshavebecomeinoperableorineffective. For the purposes of this section an amateur radio is a radio transceiver which allows the user to change or manipulate the radio frequencies used to transmit and receive messages. (Ord. text: The following words, terms, and phrases, when used in this article, shall have the meanings ascribed to them in this section, except where the context clearly indicates a different meaning: Median shall mean a physical barrier, strip of land, or a painted area separating traffic flowing in opposite directions. Pedestrian refuge shall mean a designated area within a roadway or median that is clearly marked or constructed to provide a safe space for pedestrians to stand while crossing a street. Public right-of-way shall mean the area of land acquired for or dedicated to public use for a street, highway, or alley, and shall include the roadway, shoulder, sidewalk, and median. Roadway shall mean that portion of a highway or street improved, designed, or ordinarily used for vehicular travel, exclusive of the berm or shoulder. (Ord. No. 49-2025, § 1, 10-21-2025)
breadcrumb: Div. 3. Violations > Subdiv. II. Penalties and Procedures Upon Arrest

text: It shall be unlawful for any person to engage in the following dangerous conduct within the public rights-of-way of the City of Montgomery: (1) Entering or remaining in a roadway for non-traffic purposes. No person shall enter or remain in a roadway for the purpose of giving or receiving any object, including but not limited to money, flyers, newspapers, or religious materials, from the occupant of a vehicle, or for any other purpose that is not a lawful pedestrian action as defined by state and local traffic laws. This subsection shall not apply to emergency services personnel, duly authorized public employees, or to persons with a valid permit issued by the City of Montgomery. Supp. No. 26 CD 27:29 (2) Standing, sitting, or lying on a median. No person shall stand, sit, or lie on a median, unless such median is a designated pedestrian refuge, and the person is lawfully waiting to cross a street. This provision does not apply to authorized construction or maintenance personnel. (3) Impeding the flow of traffic. No person shall, by act or omission, block, hinder, or impede the safe and orderly flow of vehicular or pedestrian traffic for any purpose other than as expressly permitted by law. This includes, but is not limited to, stepping into or remaining in the path of a moving vehicle. (4) Use of roadway when sidewalk or shoulder is available. Where a sidewalk or shoulder is provided and its use is practicable, it shall be unlawful for any pedestrian to walk along and upon an adjacent roadway. (Ord. No. 49-2025, § 2, 10-21-2025)
breadcrumb: Div. 3. Violations > Subdiv. II. Penalties and Procedures Upon Arrest

text: (a) Any person who violates the provisions of this article shall be guilty of a misdemeanor. (b) Before a citation is issued for a violation of this article, a police officer shall provide a verbal warning to the individual to cease the dangerous activity and move to a safer location. If, after receiving such a warning, the individual continues to engage in the same or similar dangerous activity, a citation may be issued. (c) This notice requirement does not apply in cases where the prohibited conduct has directly caused or is about to cause an imminent traffic accident, injury, or other emergency situation. In such cases, an officer may take immediate action as necessary for public safety. (Ord. No. 49-2025, § 3, 10-21-2025) Secs. 27-364—27-380. Reserved. ARTICLE VII. section_name: Signs or markings indicating angle parking.
text: (a) The city traffic engineer shall determine upon what streets angle parking shall be permitted and shall mark or sign such streets. Such angle parking shall not be indicated upon any federal aid or state highway within this city unless the state highway commission has determined by resolution or order entered in its minutes that the roadway is of sufficient width to permit angle parking without interfering with the movement of traffic. *State law references—Stopping, standing and parking, Code of Ala. 1975, § 32-5 A-136 et seq.; authority to regulate parking, Code of Ala. 1975, § 32-5-1(e); parking in violation of ordinance, Code of Ala. 1975, § 32-5-152; owner's responsibility for parking violations, Code of Ala. 1975, § 32-5-152.1. text: (a) The city traffic engineer is hereby authorized to determine any area in which parking is to be restricted and to indicate the restriction by means of a yellow curb. It shall be unlawful to stand or park a vehicle in any place so marked, unless otherwise designated by official signs or markings, and then in strict accordance therewith. (b) If parking in such a restricted area is indicated by spaces marked by yellow paint, the time limit for such spaces is hereby established as one hour, unless otherwise designated by an official sign and then in strict accordance therewith. If the space should be in a zone which has a time limit less than one hour, the time limit for the space shall be the same as for the zone, unless otherwise designated by an official sign, and then in strict accordance therewith. (Ord. No. 54-72, § 13-1; Code 1980, § 25-130(c)) State law reference—Authority to regulate parking, Code of Ala. 1975, § 5-1-5(e).

section_name: Parking spaces designated for persons with disabilities.
text: (a) The city's traffic engineer is hereby authorized to designate marked parking spaces within any city block for the exclusive parking of persons with long term or temporary disabilities to whom removable windshield placards or distinctive special access or disability license plates have been issued pursuant to Code of Ala. 1975, § 32-6-231. (b) Thecity'strafficengineerisauthorizedtolimittheamountoftimeindividualscanpark in spaces designated for use by persons with disabilities. Unless otherwise posted, the maximum time allowed per vehicle will be two hours. (c) It shall be unlawful for any person who does not have a distinctive special long term accessorlongtermdisabilityaccesslicenseplateorplacardortemporarydisabilityplacardas described in this section, to park a motor vehicle in a parking space designated for use by persons with disabilities. (d) Itshallbeunlawfulforanypersontoparkinaspacedesignatedforusebypersonswith disabilities for a period in excess of a time restrictions posted pursuant to this section. (e) Anypersonconvictedofviolatingthissectionshallbefined$100.00 forthefirstoffense, $300.00 for the second offense and $500.00 for the third or any subsequent offense. Supp.No.3 CD 27:32 (f) In any prosecution for any violation of the provisions of this section, it shall be prima facie evidence that the owner of such vehicle was operating the same at the time of such alleged violation, or in the case of parking overtime, that the owner parked the vehicle and caused it to be parked overtime. (Ord. No. 81-77, § 1; Code 1980, § 25-141; Ord. No. 40-82, 6-8-1982; Ord. No. 34-2001, § I, 6-21-2001; Ord. No. 25-2009, 8-4-2009)

(a) The city traffic engineer is authorized to erect signs indicating certain parking areas at the city regional airport as tow-away zones as designated and/or marked. It shall be unlawful for any unauthorized person to park a motor vehicle in any such area. (b) The city traffic engineer, those serving under his direction, or the police shall be and areherebyempoweredtoremoveanyautomobilesorothervehicleswhichareparkedorleft in the area prescribed as tow-away zones at the city regional airport. Such vehicles shall be removedfromthepremisestoalocationtobeprovidedforsuchpurposebythecityandthere held until the owner shall pay the charges for storage and removal. (c) It shall be unlawful to stop or park a motor vehicle either unattended or for a period longer than three minutes in the passenger curb loading zone at the city regional airport, andundernocircumstancesshallamotorvehiclebestoppedorparkedinthepassengercurb loading zone other than for the expeditious loading or unloading of passengers. (Ord. No. 43-2002, 8-6-2002; Ord. No. 45-2002, 8-6-2002)

section_name: Parking motor vehicles in front yards prohibited.
text: (a) Prohibition.Itisherebyordainedbythecitycouncilthatitshallhereafterbeunlawful within the corporate limits of the city to: (1) Park a motor vehicle in the front yard of a residence or business, unless the motor vehicle is parked in a driveway; (2) Parkamotorvehicleoveranypartofasidewalk,oranyunpavedportionofthepublic right-of-way. (b) Definitions. In this section the following words or phrases shall have the meaning ascribed to them in this subsection: Drivewaymeansanyareawhichisconstructedforthepurposeofparkingmotorvehicles, andwhichissurfacedwithasphalt,concrete,paversorstone. Anyareasurfacedwithpavers orstoneshallbeconstructedwithanedgingorotherboundarymaterialwhichprohibitsthe movement and spreading of the pavers or stone in order to be deemed a driveway for the purposes of this section. For the purposes of this section any driveway must connect to the public street and must include a curb cut where street curbing is present. Frontyardmeansanyportionofalotorparceloflandwhichextendsitsfullwidthandlies betweentheedgeofapublicstreetandthefrontoftheprincipalbuildingonthelotorparcel. Supp. No. 9 CD 27:33 Motorvehiclemeanseveryvehiclewhichisself-propelled,everyvehiclewhichispropelled byelectricpower,andeveryvehiclethatisdrawnbyaself-propelledvehicle,includingevery motor vehicle which is required to be registered and licensed under Alabama law. Principal building means the principal dwelling house, office, or, if there is no principal dwelling house or office, that building which is closest in distance to the public street. The front of the principal building shall be all of the principal building which lies between the two corners which constitute the widest portion of that side of the principal building which faces the public street and shall include all sides of a building which face a public street where the lot or parcel either lies in a curve of the public street, or which occupies a corner bounded on two or more sides by a public street. Registered owner means the owner of a motor vehicle as shown on the motor vehicle registration records of the state department of revenue or the analogous department or agency of another state or country. Sidewalk means any area within the public right-of-way which is surfaced with asphalt, concrete, pavers or stone, and which is set aside and maintained by the city for the purpose of accommodating pedestrian traffic. (c) Exceptions. (1) Limiteduse.Forthepurposesofthissection,parkinginthefrontyard,asrestricted bysubsection(a)(1),shallnotbeprohibitedwherethemotorvehicleisparkedforthe limited purpose of loading, unloading, or washing. This exception shall only apply whiletheowneroroperatorofthemotorvehicleisactivelyengagedinthesaidtask, and in no event shall this exception apply for more than three hours in any given 24-hour period. (2) Emergency vehicles. The prohibitions of this section shall not apply to authorized emergency vehicles parked while emergency personnel are engaged in answering a callorrenderingassistance. Authorizedemergencyvehiclesshallincludepoliceand fire vehicles, as well as ambulances and hearses. (3) Historic districts. The provisions of this section shall not operate to allow the construction of driveways or the use of property in historic districts which conflicts with the rules and regulations governing land use in those districts. (d) Authority to enforce; citation. (1) Themayor,orhisdulyappointeddesignee,shallbechargedwiththeenforcementof this section. For each violation, a citation shall be posted on or near the front windshield of the motor vehicle found to be parked in violation of this section. The registered owner of the vehicle shall have 30 calendar days within which to enter a guilty plea and pay the said fine. (2) The mayor, or his duly appointed designee, shall have the authority to enter upon privatepropertyforthepurposeofticketinganoffendingmotorvehiclefoundparked in violation of this section. Supp. No. 9 CD 27:34 (e) Authority of municipal court. The municipal court is vested with the power and jurisdictiontoadminister,hearandadjudicatethecivilviolationsandtoissuesummonsand orders imposing the civil fines, costs and fees. (f) Imposition of penalties and procedures for violations. (1) Fines.Anypersonfoundtohaveparkedinviolationofthissectionshallbesubjectto afine. Eachcalendardayinwhichaviolationhereundercontinuesorreoccursshall constitute a separate violation. a. Firstviolation.Forfirstviolation,uponconviction,thefineshallbeaminimum of $50.00. b. Second violation. For second violation within a 12-month period, upon convic- tion, the fine shall be a minimum of $150.00. c. Forthirdandallsubsequentviolations.Forthirdandanysubsequentviolation within 12-month period, upon conviction, the fine shall be a minimum of $300.00. (2) Hearing. A hearing shall be held by the municipal court at the time and place prescribed in the summons. Upon a determination by the municipal court that a violationhasoccurred,thecourtshallorderthepersonfoundtohavebeenoperating thevehicletopaythedesignatedfine. Themunicipalcourtshallhavetheadditional authority to impose any court costs which may be allowable by state law upon a conviction after hearing or upon receiving a guilty plea after the issuance of the summons. (3) Failure to pay. If, after the hearing, the municipal court orders the payment of the fine and/or costs and the person so ordered to pay fails to pay the said amounts, the municipal court may enforce said order in accordance with state, federal, or local laws which empower the court with enforcement authority. (4) Failure to appear. If the registered owner does not appear at time and place prescribedinthesummons,andfailstoenteraguiltypleaandpaythefinebeforethe said prescribed time ordered, the municipal court may enforce the said order in accordance with state, federal, or local laws which empower the court with enforcement authority. (Ord. No. 15-2009, §§ 1—4, 4-7-2009; Ord. No. 65-2012, 10-16-2012; Ord. No. 23-2016, 5-17-2016) Editor’snote—Ord. text: (a) No person shall stop, stand or park a vehicle for any purpose or period of time other than for the expeditious loading or unloading of passengers in any place marked as a passenger curb loading zone during hours when the regulations applicable to such curb loading zone are effective, and then only for a period not to exceed three minutes. text: (a) Theoperatorofabusshallnotstandorparksuchvehicleuponanystreetatanyplace other than a bus stand so designated as provided in this section. (b) The operator of a bus shall not stop such vehicle upon any street at any place for the purpose of loading and unloading passengers or their baggage other than at a bus stop, bus standorpassengerloadingzonesodesignatedasprovidedinthissection,exceptincaseofan emergency. (c) The operator of a bus shall enter a bus stop, bus stand or passenger loading zone on a public street in such a manner that the bus, when stopped to load or unload passengers or baggage, shall be in a position with the right front wheel of such vehicle not further than 18 inches from the curb and in a position so as not to unduly impede the movement of other vehicular traffic. (d) The operator of a taxicab shall not stand or park such vehicle upon any street at any place other than in a taxicab stand so designated as provided in this section. section_name: State law reference—Power of city to regulate and license vehicles for hire, Code of Ala.
text: 1975, § 11-51-101. Note—Every vehicle classified as a vehicle for hire under this article shall be kept clean, sanitary, fit and of good appearance, and in a safe condition for the transportation of passengers. Supp. No. 19 CD 6:21 Drug means a drug or other substance, or immediate precursor, which is listed in schedules I, II, III, IV, or V of the Comprehensive Drug Abuse Prevention and Control Act of 1970, codified at 21 U.S.C. § 801 et seq., as it is amended, and, for the purposes of this article, shall include ethyl alcohol or ethanol. Drug test means a hair, urine, or breath test or any other acceptable drug tested administered for the purpose of detecting the presence of drugs or alcohol, or its metabolites, in a person's bodily tissue, fluids, or products. Intoxication means the state of being affected by one or more drugs, as they are defined herein, such that mental and/or physical abilities are impaired, and shall include having a blood alcohol level of .08 percent or more as measured by either breath analysis or blood test. Limousine means a motor vehicle not operated on a fixed route with the potential seating capacity of more than five persons and no more than 15 persons used in the business of transporting passengers for hire in the city. The classification "limousine," however, shall not apply to any motor vehicle which is: (1) Classified as a taxicab. (2) Used exclusively by or under a written agreement with a hotel, motel, airport, hospital, club, or other such entity for the transportation of its members, guests, patients, or clients. (3) Operated as a bus. Passenger means a person other than the driver who is an occupant of a taxicab or limousine, and, for the purpose of this article, such person shall be presumed to be a passenger for hire. Permit means authorization issued to each individual who operates a taxicab as defined in this chapter. Person means any individual, association, partnership, limited liability company, corpora- tion, or other entity of any kind. Private motor vehicle means passenger van, including limousines, which are not permitted or licensed as a taxicab. Risk manager means the risk manager of the city or his or her designee. Taxicab means all automobiles and other vehicles, except as otherwise addressed in this article, of like construction and operation employed in the carriage of passengers for hire on the streets in the city but shall not include any bus, jitney bus, or other motor vehicle constructed for the carriage of more than seven passengers and, except as provided in section 6-363, shall not include any common carrier of passengers coming under the regulation and supervision of the state public service commission. Taximeter means an instrument or device attached to a vehicle and designed to measure mechanically or electronically the distance traveled by such vehicle, to record the times the vehicle travels or is in waiting and to indicate the fares to be charged. Supp. No. 19 CD 6:22 Waiting time means the time during which a taxicab interrupts its journey at the request of a passenger so as to allow the passenger to conduct necessary business by either remaining with the taxicab or disembarking from the taxicab. (Ord. No. 80-2007, 11-20-2007; Ord. No. 1-2016, 1-5-2016) Cross reference—Definitions generally, 1-2

section_name: Shared motorized mobility device system—license/permit process.
text: (a) It shall be unlawful for a shared motorized mobility device provider to operate a shared motorized mobility device system within the City without first obtaining a license and permit. (b) A shared motorized mobility device provider shall submit an application for a shared motorizedmobilitydevicelicensetotheriskmanagementdepartment. Theapplicationshall be processed and circulated to all applicable city departments and utilities before being presented for license agreement approval to the city council. (c) In order for the shared motorized mobility device provider to receive a permit to operate on public property or public right-of-way, the shared motorized mobility device provider must have a license agreement with the city. Following the license approval, a sharedmotorizedmobilitydeviceproviderpermitshallbeissuedbythefinancedepartment director or his/her designee. (d) The shared motorized mobility device license and permit will be effective for a period of one year and may be renewed annually. text: (a) Data requirements. (1) Informationoncustomerscollectedfromasharedmotorizedmobilitydeviceprovider in connection with the shared motorized mobility device program may not be transmitted to, processed or stored at a destination outside the United States. Informationoncustomerscollectedfromasharedmotorizedmobilitydeviceprovider shall be made available to city upon request. (2) Each motorized mobility device shall be equipped with a connected lock and smart technology equipment in order to track ridership data, which data is owned and provided by a shared motorized mobility device provider. Said data shall be made available to city upon request. (3) A shared motorized mobility device provider will provide the city with anonymized trip level data by zip code tabulation area (ZCTA) which is similar to zip code but produced by the U.S. Census rather than U.S. Postal Service. Shared motorized mobility device operators have three months from initiating service to begin delivering this data to the municipality. The data shall be reported quarterly and will include: a. Timestamp, including date and time, for beginning and end of motorized mobility device trip; b. ZCTA in which trip began; c. ZCTA in which trip ended; d. Estimated distance traveled during trip, in miles; e. Duration of trip, in hours and minutes; f. Average number of motorized mobility devices in service per month; g. Information on the adaptive motorized mobility devices used; h. Informationonthenotificationsofsafetyandmaintenanceissuesreceivedfrom customers; i. Information on accidents, if any. (b) Providershallprovideeasilyvisiblecontactinformation,includinga 24-hourtoll-free phonenumberande-mailaddress,oneachsharedmotorizedmobilitydeviceformembersof the public to make relocation requests or to report other issues with shared motorized mobility devices or to ask questions. Provider will maintain a multilingual website, call center, and/or mobile app customer interface that is available 24 hours a day, seven days a week. Supp. No. 15 CD 27:62
breadcrumb: Art. X. Automated Photographic Enforcement of Traffic Control Device Violations

text: (a) The city reserves the right to identify parking areas and no-parking areas through administrative regulations. All parking areas and no-parking areas will be communicated directly to permitted operators. The city will coordinate the selection and approval of location(s) for scooter parking with scooter companies or providers. (b) Designated scooter parking, dockless or demarcated parking spaces with light torch interventions is required for all scooter operators, companies or providers for the manage- ment of scooter parking when scooters are not in use by patrons. (c) Permitted shared mobility devices shall be parked upright at all times. (d) Withindesignatedparkinglocations,whenoneispresentwithin 500 feetoftheendof trip. Supp. No. 15 CD 27:64 (e) No person shall park a permitted shared mobility device or a mobility device other than upon the sidewalk in a bike rack or against a building or at a curb, in such manner as toaffordtheleastobstructionofpedestriantrafficandprovidesaminimumoffivefeetclear for pedestrians. (f) Permitted shared mobility devices and mobility devices shall not be parked: (1) On vegetation; (2) On any grate, manhole cover, or access lid; (3) In any loading zone, driveway, vehicle travel lane, bike lane, or shared-use path; (4) Where they obstruct vehicle parking; (5) Where it obscures any fixed, regulatory, or informational signs or traffic control devices; (6) Where it obstructs emergency service infrastructure; (7) Within a traffic island, median or traffic circle; (8) On any bridge, bridge ramp, or bridge approach; (9) Within five feet of any fire hydrant; (10) Within five feet of any crosswalk; (11) Within five feet of any driveway, alley, or curb cut; (12) Within five feet of any portion of a curb ramp; (13) Within five feet of a marked disabled parking space; (14) Within five feet of a marked loading zone; (15) Where they obstruct pedestrian or wheelchair access to: a. Private or public buildings and establishments; b. Street features that require pedestrian access including, but not limited to, benches, parklets, parking pay stations, and crosswalk buttons; c. Accessibilityandsafetyfeaturesincludingcurbramps,wheelchairramps,ramp landings, handrails, areas of refuge, and detectable warning strips. (Ord. No. 24-2022, § 5, 6-21-2022)
breadcrumb: Art. XIII. Shared Motorized Mobility Device Operations

text: (a) All permitted shared mobility devices shall have permanently affixed contact information and unique provider specific device identifiers on all devices for relocation requests. The location and size of this information must be approved by the city and consistent with any size and location requirements as set forth in the administrative regulations. Operators must monitor and document relocation requests and responses 24/7. Permitted operators will remove improperly parked shared mobility devices in accordance with local laws and without prior notice from the city. Supp. No. 15 CD 27:65 (b) Nothing in this section shall preclude the city from the right to cite, impound, store, and dispose of improperly parked permitted devices at the operators' expense without the necessity of giving notice as defined herein below. (c) Asharedmotorizedmobilitydeviceprovidershallprovideamechanismforcustomers and the public to notify the provider that there is a safety or maintenance issue with the motorized mobility device. The records of all such notifications and subsequent resolutions, if any, may be reasonably requested by the city. (d) A shared motorized mobility device provider agrees to educate customers regarding lawsapplicabletoridingandoperatingamotorizedmobilitydeviceandtootherwisecomply with applicable laws. (e) A shared motorized mobility device provider shall offer its customers safety training instruction either live or via recording. (f) A shared motorized mobility device provider shall ensure motorized mobility devices have the following safety features: (1) Motorized mobility devices are equipped with wheel brakes that will enable the customer to make safe stops on dry, level, clean pavement. (2) Motorized mobility devices are equipped with a front light that emits white light while the motorized mobility device is in motion. (3) Motorized mobility devices are equipped with a solid or flashing red light and a built-in reflector on the rear. (4) Motorizedmobilitydevicesarepoweredbyabatterywithaninternationalstandard rating of IP 68 or higher. (g) Nothing in this section shall preclude a shared motorized mobility device provider or otherprivateentitiesfromobtainingthenecessaryapprovalstoconstructparkingspaceson private property or licensing public property for the construction of parking for motorized mobility device sharing. (Ord. No. 24-2022, § 6, 6-21-2022)
breadcrumb: Art. XIII. Shared Motorized Mobility Device Operations

section_name: Criteria for the location of stations.
text: (a) Noparkingspaceshallinterferewiththereasonablevisibilityneedsofdriversonthe street where the station is located or any connecting street. (b) No parking space shall interfere with the flow of pedestrian traffic taking into consideration the queuing areas for adjacent businesses, the size of the parking space and the width of the sidewalk. (c) No parking space shall be located so as to unreasonably interfere with businesses located on the same block (block faces) as the proposed station location. Supp. No. 15 CD 27:66 (d) Criteriaforapprovalofthelocationofparkingspacesmayinclude,butnotbelimited to, compatibility with other existing city plans and goals, for example, the downtown plan, the comprehensive plan, and the motorized mobility device/pedestrian plan. (Ord. No. 24-2022, § 7, 6-21-2022)
breadcrumb: Art. XIII. Shared Motorized Mobility Device Operations

section_name: Shared motorized mobility device provider responsible for condition of public property.
text: All parking spaces located on public property are installed at the risk of the shared motorized mobility device provider who shall at all times have the obligation to return the public property on which the parking space is located back to the city in the same condition as when the license agreement was entered into. (Ord. No. 24-2022, § 8, 6-21-2022)
breadcrumb: Art. XIII. Shared Motorized Mobility Device Operations

text: The shared motorized mobility device provider shall be responsible for maintaining the parking spaces and property to which the parking spaces are secured in a safe and clean condition. The stations shall be kept in good order and repair at all times. (Ord. No. 24-2022, § 9, 6-21-2022)
breadcrumb: Art. XIII. Shared Motorized Mobility Device Operations
